name: Ekpyrotic / cyclic universe
summary: Replaces inflation with a slow contracting phase preceding the hot Big Bang. In heterotic M-theory, a 5D bulk separates two parallel 4D branes; a slow approach and collision of these branes generates the hot Big Bang. The contracting phase produces a near-scale-invariant perturbation spectrum without exponential expansion, addressing the horizon and flatness problems differently from inflation.

addresses_puzzles.note: Solves horizon, flatness, monopole problems via contracting phase; perturbations from ekpyrotic collapse produce a nearly scale-invariant spectrum. Predicts very small tensor-to-scalar ratio r ~ 10^-6 (no GW signal in CMB-S4 reach), distinguishing from inflation. Conceptual issues with passing through the brane collision (singularity / bounce) remain unresolved.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts r << 0.01, well below LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 sensitivity. Detection of r ~ 0.001 would falsify ekpyrotic models.
Predicts specific non-Gaussianity (large local f_NL) that distinguishes from inflation.

introduces.note: Brane potential, ekpyrotic energy scale, bounce physics, perturbation generation parameters.

history: Khoury, Ovrut, Steinhardt, Turok (2001), 'The Ekpyrotic Universe: Colliding Branes and the Origin of the Hot Big Bang', PRD 64, 123522, arXiv:hep-th/0103239. Cyclic-universe extension by Steinhardt-Turok (2002). Active competitor to inflation in cosmology debates; observational distinguishability remains the key open question.

references.citation: Khoury, Ovrut, Steinhardt, Turok, 'The Ekpyrotic Universe: Colliding Branes and the Origin of the Hot Big Bang', Phys. Rev. D 64, 123522 (2001)
